p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot, dense foliage, trailing habit.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Remained stable throughout the sequence; foliage density is consistent.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_inference: High turgidity, no signs of chlorosis or wilting.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Centralized in black pot, two primary leaves, secondary pair emerging.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: Shows slight leaf curling over the 5-day period, likely due to ambient humidity fluctuations.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Stable, though leaf margins show minor dehydration stress.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Two leaves, white rabbit anchor present.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The apical leaf has shown a slight downward droop (approx 3mm) compared to the earliest image.
p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Moderate stress; leaf tip necrosis is visible on the larger leaf.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Smallest specimen, located near the rim of the p2/p4 shared pot.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Growth is stagnant; no new leaf production observed over the 5-day window.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears consistently dry with minor surface cracking in p2/p4.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Minor debris (dust/particulate) on the desk surface near the yellow pot.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: The sequence shows a gradual decline in soil moisture levels across all pots, leading to the current state of slight leaf drooping in p3.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: Overall biome health is 'Fair'. The primary concern is the progressive dehydration of the soil medium, which is beginning to impact the turgor pressure of p3.
vision_report.anomalies: None detected beyond standard indoor plant senescence.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a sequential audit from the earliest image to the current state. The plants are generally stable, but the soil moisture levels have dropped, leading to visible stress in the Pothos (p3). The String of Nickels (p1) remains the most robust specimen. I have validated these findings against the chronological sequence.
